batch_5_copy_templates.status: READY — awaiting Ryedale raw data for per-lead personalisation.
batch_5_copy_templates.send_date_target: TUESDAY 2026-05-06 — from 10:00 (after any fu_041-045 contingency sends)
batch_5_copy_templates.instruction: HANDS: return raw Ryedale scrape data. BRAIN personalises per lead on data receipt. Do NOT use these templates directly.

subject_examples: Mark — your Malton plumbing site is flagged 'Not Secure'
Julie — your Pickering B&B website is flagged 'Not Secure'
David — your Helmsley estate agency site isn't secure
Helen — your Kirkbymoorside joinery site is flagged 'Not Secure'
Paul — your Norton garage site isn't secure
template_ssl_pas.body_template:
[First name],

I was looking at [business name]'s website just now — [specific URL].

Chrome is flagging it as 'Not Secure' because it's running on HTTP rather than HTTPS. Any customer who clicks through from Google or a recommendation will see that warning before they even read your homepage.

For a [trade/business type] in [town], that trust signal matters — particularly if customers are comparing you with competitors before calling.

I fix this sort of thing for small businesses across Yorkshire. It's usually a one-day job and costs less than you'd think.

Worth a quick chat?

[Your name]
template_ssl_pas.day7_new_angle: Google ranking impact of Not Secure OR missing/incomplete Google Business Profile. NEVER repeat SSL angle.
template_ssl_pas.notes: Confirmed winner: 3/3 INTERESTED replies used this exact pattern. Do not deviate from structure.

subject_examples: Paul — your Kirkbymoorside joinery site doesn't work on mobile
Sarah — your Norton restaurant site is hard to use on a phone
James — your Hovingham B&B site doesn't load properly on a phone
template_mobile_pas.body_template:
[First name],

I checked [business name]'s website on a mobile just now — [specific URL].

The text is tiny, the layout breaks on a smaller screen, and the phone number isn't tap-to-call. Anyone searching for a [trade] in [town] on their phone — which is most people — will likely give up and try the next result.

I help small businesses across Yorkshire fix exactly this. It's usually a half-day job.

Would it help to take a look?

[Your name]
template_mobile_pas.day7_new_angle: SSL (if HTTP), OR missing click-to-call, OR specific local competitor who IS mobile-friendly.
template_mobile_pas.notes: Secondary signal. Tom's conversion came Day-7, not Day-0. Mobile needs the new angle to fire.

template_no_website_bab.qualification: 4★+ AND 20+ reviews (Ryedale — rural market, lowered from 50+ in Batch 4)
template_no_website_bab.subject_pattern: [Business name] — [town] [trade]: customers can't find you online
subject_examples: Malton Plumbing — customers searching for a plumber in Malton can't find you online
Pickering Joiners — no website means Google can't recommend you
Helmsley Builders — your competitors with websites are getting calls you're missing
template_no_website_bab.body_template:
Hi,

I searched for '[trade] [town]' on Google just now — [business name] doesn't appear.

Customers who don't already know you will find your competitors instead. In a market like [town], the first business with a credible website often gets the call.

A simple one-page website — your services, your area, a contact form — is usually enough to start ranking locally within a few weeks.

Would that be useful?

[Your name]
template_no_website_bab.day7_new_angle: Competitors who ARE ranking for the search term. Or estimate of missed enquiries ('A plumber in Malton with a basic website typically picks up 3-5 enquiries a month from search').
template_no_website_bab.notes: 0% INTERESTED at 50+ reviews in Batch 4. Lowering to 20+ for rural Ryedale market. Reference Google Maps listing specifically.

template_aida_keyword_gap.subject_pattern: [First name] — [N] reviews but '[keyword] [town]' isn't bringing your site up
subject_examples: Helen — 127 reviews but 'accountant Helmsley' isn't bringing up your site
James — your pub has 200+ reviews but doesn't rank for 'pub Pickering'
template_aida_keyword_gap.body_template:
[First name],

[Business name] has [N] reviews — that's a strong local reputation.

But when I searched '[keyword] [town]' on Google just now, your website didn't appear in the top results. Your competitors with fewer reviews are ranking above you because their sites are technically better optimised.

The gap is fixable — usually a few hours of on-page SEO work and a small structural change.

Would it be worth a look?

[Your name]
template_aida_keyword_gap.day7_new_angle: Page speed score OR missing LocalBusiness structured data OR specific competitor comparison.
template_aida_keyword_gap.notes: Keyword-gap pivot from Batch 4. Test: specific keyword name ('plumber Malton shows X and Y above you').
